div.head: The Chargé in the United Kingdom (Gallman) to the Secretary of State
div.p:
9392. Accompanied by Denby we discussed the pricing of individual items of reciprocal aid with CadoganSir Alexander Cadogan, British Permanent Under Secretary of State for Foreign Affairs. yesterday and today Reed, Edgerton, Maxwell,James A. Maxwell, Office of Wartime Economic Affairs. Denby, and HawkinsHarry C. Hawkins, Economic Counselor of the American Embassy. discussed the subject further with Sir John Anderson (re Department’s 8872 [8972?], October 27, 3 p.m.). The British officials referred to showed appreciation of our position and an inclination to cooperate. Anderson
brought in Sir Wilfred EadyJoint Second Secretary, British Treasury. who will have direct charge of the matter and the question was further briefly discussed with him. Eady indicated that over a large part of the field price data are available and would be furnished. As for the rest the question of what could be done would be explored in detail. Arrangements were made for a meeting tomorrow with Eady and members of his staff at which detailed work will begin and an effort will be made to complete it as soon as possible.
